title: Add task_budget advisory for Morty delegation to prevent runaway agentic loops
source_context: delegate.py:929 delegate_to_agent has timeout but no token-loop budget. For long agentic Morty runs this causes unpredictable cost/token usage. The claude CLI doesn't yet accept --task-budget natively, but we can inject it via prompt scaffolding until CLI support lands.
description: Opus 4.7 introduced task_budgets (beta header task-budgets-2026-03-13) — an advisory token budget the model sees as a running countdown across a full agentic loop. Unlike max_tokens (hard cap, model unaware), task_budget lets the model self-moderate and gracefully finish work. PERFECT for Morty subprocess delegation where Morty's can currently run unbounded. Implementation: add --task-budget flag to delegate.py; when set, inject a TASK BUDGET section into the Morty system prompt: 'You have approximately N tokens across this entire agentic loop (thinking + tool calls + output). Prioritize and wrap up gracefully as you approach this limit.' Map ticket complexity to budget: XL=200k, L=120k, M=60k, S=30k. Minimum 20k per Anthropic docs.
proposed_changes: Add task_budget param to delegate_to_agent(). In the Morty prompt assembly, prepend: '## BUDGET (Opus 4.7 task_budget advisory)\nYou have ~{N} tokens total for this entire agentic loop (thinking, tool calls, final output). Track your usage mentally and finish gracefully as the budget approaches exhaustion.\n'. Add --task-budget CLI arg, auto-derive from ticket complexity if unset.
